response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js web and platform development. While Java appears in his skills list and surfaces briefly at National Instruments (C/C++ IDE tooling, 2014–2015) and Altom Consulting (robotic arms, 2016–2018), there is no evidence of meaningful Java or Kotlin depth in any recent role, no mention of Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate, and no JVM internals exposure. The only IDE tooling experience is a decade old, was C-based rather than JVM-based, and does not translate to the enterprise IDE subsystem ownership this position requires.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) consistently applied across multiple companies
Demonstrated experience building developer tooling — test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), MCP code generators, and brief C IDE work at National Instruments
Proven ability to operate in high-throughput, complex systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
concerns: Primary technical stack for the past 10+ years is TypeScript/Node.js with no recent Java or Kotlin production work
No mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework anywhere in the resume
IDE internals experience is a single C/C++ role from 2014–2015 with no JVM or language-tooling relevance

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise JVM framework exposure
